description_long: This is a dried, wilted rose flower with tightly curled, overlapping petals that have taken on a deep reddish-pink hue. The petals are crinkled and desiccated, showing signs of age and dehydration, with some edges appearing slightly brown and brittle. The flower head is compact and somewhat asymmetrical, with the outer petals curling outward and downward, while the inner petals remain densely packed. A slender, woody stem extends from the base, also showing signs of dryness and fragility, and a few withered sepals are visible at the base of the bloom. No leaves are present, and the overall appearance is that of a flower past its prime, often seen as a keepsake or remnant in urban environments such as sidewalks, parks, or near memorials. The coloration is dominated by muted reds and pinks, with subtle gradients of brown and purple from the drying process.
description: A dried, wilted rose flower with curled, deep reddish-pink petals and a slender, brittle stem. The petals are crinkled and layered, showing signs of age and dehydration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the densely packed, curled petals of the rose, with the central spiral and most of the flower's face visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered, crinkled petals and the slender, woody stem extending upward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the flower head, with the stem and some withered sepals visible, and petals curling away from the center.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the overlapping petals from the side and front, emphasizing the flower's asymmetry and texture.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a spiral configuration, densely overlapping and layered with no clear radial symmetry. The outer petals curl outward and downward, while the inner petals remain tightly packed, creating a complex, whorled appearance typical of a rose.
